finding: Slovak firms' upside inflation risk balance widened further above the euro area
bullets: A net 61.23% of Slovak firms (n=242) saw upside risks to five-year inflation expectations in wave 39, up 2.30 pp from a net 58.93% in wave 38 — suggesting firms anticipate persistent price pressures.
The SK–EA gap widened to 7.2 pp in wave 39, as Slovak firms' balance rose while the euro area's eased 1.28 pp to a net 54.04% (n=5,087) — highlighting diverging inflation concerns.
Euro area firms' upside inflation risk balance edged down from a net 55.32% in wave 38, placing Slovakia as the more inflation-risk-concerned economy.
chart_subtitle: Slovak firms' upside inflation risk balance reached net 61.23%.
